Version 1/ 2 Instrument maintenance instructions 20.9.2020 
 
Instructions for sterilizing at Biomedicum 1 and 2U 
 
If you have any wishes regarding the products, for example , you need certain products to be sterilized, please 
send a request to our joint email: 
valinehuolto -meilahti@helsinki.fi 
When you send us a message, please remember to tell us which lab you are worki ng in, which floor your lab is 
located on (including which house) , and your contact information so that we can contact you if needed. 
 
Products to be sterilized 
The products will be ready at the earliest the next day . 
• The expiry date of the product is indicated on the pro ducts . In the case of two dates , the earlier date 
is the date of sterilization. 
 
o Incubator shelves are sterilized only for old incubators that do not have a decontamination 
program. (Of course, we wash the shelves of all incubators ). 
o Certain items are automatically sterilized after washing, for example , ultracentrifuge tubes 
and instruments. These products are marked by equipment maintenance using autoclav able 
marking tapes. 
 
Do the following: 
• If you need general glassware to be sterilized (eg , Erlenmeyers , etc.) , send us a service request by 
email. We will bring you the product you requested , as soon as we can , to the service point nearest 
you.

sterilized (eg , Erlenmeyers , etc.) , send us a service request by 
email. We will bring you the product you requested , as soon as we can , to the service point nearest 
you. 
 
• If you wish to have your clean items (eg , Eppendorf tubes , etc.) back sterilized , bring them in 
sterilizing bags to the nearest service point . 
o Fill the bag only up to 60% . Otherwise , steam may not disperse around all products and 
sterilize them properly . Also , the bag must have space for the seams . 
o The text should be at the end of the bag, i.e., outside the seams, not in the sterile region 
o Be sure to send the tip boxes to be wash ed from time to time . 
Avoid the following: 
o Putting dirty items needing to be washed among clean things to be sterilized, because we 
have to assume that all items are contaminated and rewash them all. 
o Putting a clean product to be sterilized (eg , sterilization bag) in a dirty washbasin . The product 
is contaminated , and we have to dispose of the bag and its goods in the trash. 
o To bag a dirty pr oduct in an autoclave bag . Please follow the instructions above. We assume 
that the bagged items are clean and ready for sterilization.

Version 1/ 2 Instrument maintenance instructions 20.9.2020 
Liquids to be sterilized 
Bring a ll liquids to be sterilized to the service point , where the service personnel will retrieve them . 
 
• Always mark on the bottle what the solution is . The personnel will not autoclave unknown liquids . 
(Quality and safety issue). Also , mark who’s it is (Lab name and location , for example , lab A, 2U, 3 floor ) so 
that we know where to return it. 
 
o Fill bottles with only 70% of the bottle volume . Otherwise , the liquids will boil over in the 
autoclave. 
o Critical concentration solutions , such as 10XPBS can be filled up to 85% volume, because they are 
run with a slower, more water -consuming program. Before the delivery of the bottles for 
sterilization , contact us to discuss the matter via our email. 
o DEPC waters and DEPC -flushed, bagged products are also brought to a service point . They are not 
picked up by laboratory technicians from fume hoods . (DEPC evaporates CO 2 and ethanol in small 
amounts so that they can be safely taken to a service point) . 
 
Marking the products: 
• If you use your own printed stickers on the caps, please usen dry -heat indicator tape beneath it. 
Otherwise , the stickers are very hard to remove. 
• When you mark your bottles do not “go wild” with tape. Please see the examples below.
